name: urban_policy_municipal_bond_covenant_harbor_car_fair
premise: A Urban Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving urban policy municipal bond covenant harbor car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Soren Chandra
agents.identity: I am Soren Chandra, accountable for a defensible call in Urban Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Keisha Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Keisha Kowalski, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Urban Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Soren Chandra must decide under care vs fairness. What does Soren Chandra do?
